explanations.1: This line means 'the file composer.lock depends on composer.json', so if composer.json changes, this rule will run.
explanations.2: The ${CMD} is a variable that holds a command prefix (like 'docker exec' or similar); it runs composer update to refresh dependencies based on composer.json.
explanations.4: This line means 'the vendor directory depends on composer.lock', so whenever composer.lock changes, this rule installs the exact versions listed in that lock file.
explanations.5: The ${CMD} runs composer install, which reads composer.lock and installs the locked versions of all packages into the vendor folder.
